events.info: The empire reaches its greatest extent under King Ezana.
The start of the massive stone city-state's rise.
Kilwa becomes the dominant city-state on the Swahili Coast.
Portuguese explorer arrives in Mombasa and Malindi.
Sultan Seyyid Said moves his capital from Muscat to Zanzibar.
European powers divide Africa, triggering the 'Scramble'.
IBEA Company is granted rights to administer British East Africa.
Ethiopia defeats Italy, maintaining its independence.
Unified uprising against German colonial rule in Tanganyika.
Early political mobilization against colonial labor laws in Kenya.
Armed struggle against British land policies in Kenya.
Detention of Jomo Kenyatta and five other independence leaders.
Julius Nyerere leads Tanganyika to sovereign rule.
Jomo Kenyatta becomes the first Prime Minister.
Overthrow of the Sultan and establishment of a republic.
Regional integration between Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania.
